_doc: Methodology pin. The single declaration of every input and parameter that affects analytical output. Every artifact (snapshot, briefing, metrics, analysis, draft) is stamped with `meta_version` so longitudinal consumers can tell which era it belongs to. Bump rules: patch=no output change; minor=forward-compatible (added story, added feed); major=invalidates longitudinal comparison (changed Jaccard formula, swapped embedding model, removed buckets). Bump via `python baseline_pin.py --bump <level> --reason '...'`. CI enforces hash match via `python -m meta` on every PR.
pin_reason: Phase C: HDBSCAN clustering (replaces DBSCAN), 10 long-running dossier patterns + n_buckets floor lowered to 3 + auto_promote module, population x audience-reach bucket weighting + weighted_frame_distribution, Common Crawl News paywall fallback, RSS-vs-sitemap audit script + COVERAGE.md gap-disclosure

tokenizer._doc: Unicode-aware tokenizer via the `regex` library. \p{L} matches any Unicode letter so diacritics in translated text (e.g. 'México', 'résumé') are preserved as single tokens. Lower bound 4 chars matches the prior pin to keep min vocabulary size stable. Operates on the English-pivot field signal_text_en (see meta.effective_text); only English content tokens flow into Jaccard / TF-IDF / exclusive-vocab metrics.

clustering._doc: Primary clusterer is HDBSCAN as of meta_version 6.0.0 (Phase C.5). Density-adaptive; no eps to justify; exposes per-cluster stability scores via cluster_topics.last_stability_scores. Falls back to legacy DBSCAN (eps + min_samples below) when hdbscan isn't installed. Calibration: see archive/clustering_calibration_*.md. The k-distance plot used to justify the prior DBSCAN eps=0.35 is no longer authoritative; HDBSCAN derives the equivalent threshold per-density.

metrics._doc: Cross-bucket lexical metrics. As of 5.0.0 the primary similarity is TF-IDF cosine over per-bucket pivot-language vocabularies (frequency-weighted, length-normalized). Token-set Jaccard is preserved as a legacy field for diff-runs. LaBSE embedding cosine on ORIGINAL signal_text is published as a parallel cross-lingual semantic check (model-pinned, optional dependency).

ensemble._doc: Multi-LLM ensemble agreement on closed-codebook frame_id selections (analytical/ensemble.py). Krippendorff alpha gates publication: >= alpha_gate_ship → ship, >= alpha_gate_preliminary → ship with preliminary flag, otherwise suppress.

canary._doc: Drift-canary suite (canary/run.py). Runs eight frozen (article, instruction) pairs against the pinned analyzer model on every cron and compares to the most recent canary/baseline/. Categorical primary_frame match rate < 0.75 OR mean output cosine < 0.92 raises a soft warning in the job summary.

claude._doc: Dated snapshot ID (claude-sonnet-4-6-YYYYMMDD) pins model weights as far as the API permits — alias `claude-sonnet-4-6` may roll forward as Anthropic ships updates. Anthropic deprecates snapshots after ~12 months; bump major when a snapshot is end-of-life and re-baseline. Drift between snapshot and what is actually served is detected by canary/run.py (see meta.canary).

translation._doc: Pivot-language translation step (analytical/translate.py). Every non-English signal_text is translated to English via Claude before metrics are computed; signal_text_en + title_en are added per corpus entry. Originals are preserved verbatim for citation grounding. Cache: cache/translations/<sha256[:2]>/<sha256>.json keyed on (model, lang, text). Bumping the model invalidates the cache by design.
